title: Electrical service and maintenance mechanics and repairers
description: Research, design, develop, or test computer or computer-related equipment for commercial, industrial, military, or scientific use. May supervise the manufacturing and installation of computer or computer-related equipment and components. Research, design, develop, or test electronic components and systems for commercial, industrial, military, or scientific use employing knowledge of electronic theory and materials properties. Design electronic circuits and components for use in fields such as telecommunications, aerospace guidance and propulsion control, acoustics, or instruments and controls. Design and implement radio frequency identification device (RFID) systems used to track shipments or goods. (Based on 33 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Direct or train workers to install, maintain, or repair electrical wiring, equipment, or fixtures.
Install ground leads and connect power cables to equipment, such as motors, and test connections for compatibility and safety.
Inspect electrical systems, equipment, or components to identify hazards, defects, and ensure compliance with codes.
Advise management on the potential hazards of continuing operation of malfunctioning equipment.
Maintain current electrician's license or identification card to comply with governmental regulations.
Plan layout and installation of electrical wiring and fixtures according to job specifications and local codes.
Assemble, install, test, or maintain electrical or electronic wiring, equipment, appliances, or fixtures, using appropriate tools.
Repair or replace wiring, equipment, or fixtures using hand or power tools, and diagnose malfunctioning systems to correct problems.
Work from ladders, scaffolds, or roofs and use appropriate safety practices while installing, maintaining, or repairing systems.
Place conduits, pipes, or tubing inside designated areas and pull insulated wires or cables to complete circuits between boxes.
Perform physically demanding tasks such as digging trenches for cable installation or moving heavy objects.
Test electrical systems and test conductors to ensure compatibility and prevent incorrect connections.
Construct or fabricate parts, using hand tools according to specifications and assembling communication equipment like data and telephone lines.
Inspect and test power lines and auxiliary equipment to locate and identify problems, using testing instruments.
Climb poles or use vehicles to access equipment, and test operation of transmission components using appropriate tools.
Clean, maintain, and organize tools and test equipment to ensure proper functioning and safety.
Install, service, and repair electronic equipment such as televisions and radios, and provide assistance in emergencies as needed.
Diagnose and repair faulty equipment, and provide customers with instructions on proper use and care of equipment.
Keep records of work orders and maintenance reports, and prepare sketches or follow blueprints during installations.
Communicate effectively with customers, explaining repairs and obtaining necessary approvals for work.
Fabricate parts and test aids as required.
Assemble components such as switches, electrical controls, and junction boxes, using hand tools or soldering irons.
Keep records of maintenance and repair work performed on appliances and report information about mileage or track inspected, repairs performed, and equipment requiring replacement.
Adjust, repair, or replace malfunctioning components or assemblies, using hand tools or soldering irons.
Connect components to assemblies such as radio systems, instruments, and magnetos, using hand tools and soldering irons.
Install electrical and electronic components and systems in aircraft, using hand tools, power tools, or soldering irons.
Analyze user needs, evaluate specifications, confer with engineering staff, and recommend appropriate hardware.
Test and verify hardware and support peripherals to ensure they meet specifications by recording and analyzing test data.
Provide training and support to system designers and users, and technical support to marketing and sales departments.
Inspect, maintain, and replace batteries, and perform tests to diagnose malfunctions in electrical units and components.
Repair and rebuild defective mechanical parts in electric motors, generators, and related equipment, using hand tools and power tools.
Disassemble defective equipment and appliances to diagnose problems and make repairs, using hand tools.
Operate lifts, hoists, or chain falls to move heavy parts or equipment during installation or repair processes.
Lubricate moving parts to ensure proper functioning and reduce wear in equipment and machinery.
Inspect installation sites and study blueprints and work orders to determine materials and installation procedures.
Test circuits, sensors, and equipment to locate faults and ensure they conform to specifications and safety standards.
Replace worn or defective parts such as switches, bearings, and defective wiring, ensuring compliance with specifications.
Keep informed of new products and advancements in technology to maintain current knowledge in electrical service and maintenance.
Set appliance thermostats and check to ensure that they are functioning properly.
Inspect and test operation, mechanical parts, and circuitry of gate crossings, signals, and signal equipment such as interlocks and hotbox detectors.
Install, inspect, maintain, and repair various railroad service equipment on the road or in the shop, including railroad signal systems.
Take measurements to determine if appliances will fit in installation locations and perform minor carpentry work when necessary for proper installation.
Measure, cut, and thread pipe, and connect it to feeder lines and equipment or appliances using rules and hand tools.
Test and examine gas pipelines and equipment to locate leaks, faulty connections, and to determine pressure and flow of gas.
Light and adjust pilot lights on gas stoves and examine valves and burners for gas leakage and specified flame.
Contact supervisors or offices to receive repair assignments.
Install appliances such as refrigerators, washing machines, and stoves.
Respond to emergency calls for problems such as gas leaks.
Install gas pipes and water lines to connect appliances to existing lines or plumbing.
Level washing machines and connect hoses to water pipes using hand tools.
Clean, lubricate, and touch up minor defects on newly installed or repaired appliances.
Instruct customers regarding operation and care of appliances and provide emergency service information.
Refer to schematic drawings, product manuals, and troubleshooting guides to diagnose and repair problems.
Observe and test operation of appliances following installation and make any initial installation adjustments that are necessary.
Connect electrical wiring to control panels and electric motors.
Inspect wiring connections, control panel hookups, and alignments of cars and hoistways to ensure that equipment operates properly.
Perform routine maintenance such as cleaning and oiling parts to detect and prevent problems.
Repair, adjust, or replace electrical or mechanical components or parts using hand tools, power tools, or soldering equipment.
Record repairs made, time spent, and parts used.
Maintain records of equipment maintenance work or repairs, including performance problems and parts used.
Examine work orders and converse with equipment operators to detect equipment problems and identify contributing factors.
Operate machines to test the functioning of parts or mechanisms and demonstrate proper use to operators.
Travel to customers' locations to service machines or provide emergency repairs and advise customers concerning equipment operation and maintenance.
Reinstall software programs or adjust settings on existing software to fix machine malfunctions and calibrate testing instruments.
Assemble machines according to specifications, using hand or power tools and measuring devices, and disassemble machines to examine parts for defects.
Inspect components of industrial equipment to ensure accurate assembly, installation, or identify defects, such as loose connections or frayed wires.
Clean, oil, or adjust mechanical parts to maintain machines' operating efficiency and to prevent breakdowns, including routine maintenance work.
Maintain inventories of spare parts for all equipment, research, order, and replenish parts inventory as necessary.
Repair or replace defective parts, including electrical components, using hand tools or power tools, and conduct troubleshooting as needed.
Test faulty equipment to diagnose malfunctions and ensure that repaired systems are operating efficiently.
Schedule maintenance for industrial machines and equipment, prepare maintenance schedules, and maintain service records.
Direct or coordinate activities for the installation, maintenance, or modification of electronic equipment and systems.
Collaborate with other workers for the installation or maintenance of equipment and train users or technicians on operation and safety.
Dismantle machinery using hand tools for repairs and reassemble equipment after completion of inspections or maintenance.
Operate equipment to demonstrate proper use and conduct routine evaluations of machinery to identify operational issues.
Analyze test results, machine error messages, or feedback from operators to diagnose equipment problems effectively.
Weld, repair, and fabricate equipment or machinery to maintain or enhance functionality and safety standards.
Inspect electronic equipment and instruments to ensure compliance with specifications, safety standards, or relevant regulations.
Test and replace electrical components and wiring using appropriate equipment and tools for measurements and repairs.
Repair bent or torn sheet metal and electrical equipment.
Adjust repaired or replaced units as needed to ensure proper operation and perform scheduled maintenance on equipment.
Repair, fabricate, and install components using blueprints, shop sketches, and instruction manuals.
Disassemble units such as water pumps, control valves, and compressors for repairs and test electrical systems using testing equipment.
Replace defective wiring and insulation, tighten connections, and install new electrical equipment using hand tools.
Adjust, repair, or replace defective wiring and relays in various systems using electrician's tools.
Locate and remove or repair circuit defects and inspect electrical systems to diagnose malfunctions.
Reassemble and test equipment after repairs and maintain equipment service records.
Monitor and inspect work areas, tools, and equipment to ensure worker safety and compliance with safety rules.
Meet with vendors to discuss products for repair work and estimate costs based on parts and labor.
Conduct worker training in safety, repair, and maintenance techniques and evaluate maintenance policies and procedures.
Confer with management and personnel to coordinate work activities and identify resource needs.
